question_text: Foot and Mouth Disease (FMD) of cattle, sheep and pigs is caused by which specific class of microorganism?
explanation: Foot and Mouth Disease is caused by the FMD virus, a member of the genus Aphthovirus in the family Picornaviridae. It is a highly contagious viral disease affecting cloven-hoofed animals, characterised by fever and vesicles on the tongue, mouth, feet and teats. There are seven serotypes including O, A and Asia-1, important in India.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

options.text: A spore-forming bacterium of the genus Bacillus
A virus belonging to the family Picornaviridae
A protozoan parasite spread through tick bites
A filamentous fungus growing inside the hooves

elimination_hints.hint: Bacillus causes anthrax, not FMD
Tick-borne protozoa cause babesiosis or theileriosis
FMD is not a fungal infection

question_text: Anthrax in cattle is caused by which specific spore-forming bacterium that survives for decades in the soil?
explanation: Anthrax is caused by Bacillus anthracis, a Gram-positive, rod-shaped, spore-forming bacterium. The spores are extremely resistant and can survive in soil for decades, making contaminated pastures a long-term hazard. Anthrax is a notifiable disease and a serious zoonosis transmissible to humans handling infected carcasses.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

options.text: Mycobacterium tuberculosis var. bovis
Pasteurella multocida serotype B:2
Bacillus anthracis spore-forming bacterium
Clostridium chauvoei of soil origin

elimination_hints.hint: Mycobacterium causes bovine tuberculosis, not anthrax
Pasteurella causes haemorrhagic septicaemia
Clostridium chauvoei causes black quarter, not anthrax

question_text: Mastitis in dairy cows is most accurately described as inflammation of which specific organ of the animal?
explanation: Mastitis is the inflammation of the mammary gland or udder of the dairy cow, almost always due to bacterial infection through the teat canal. It is the most economically important disease of dairy cattle worldwide because it reduces milk yield, alters milk quality with clots and blood, and shortens productive life of cows.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

options.text: Inflammation of the lungs and respiratory tract
Inflammation of the mammary gland or udder
Inflammation of the joints of the hind legs
Inflammation of the small intestine and colon

elimination_hints.hint: Lung inflammation is pneumonia
Joint inflammation is arthritis
Intestinal inflammation is enteritis

question_text: Which of the following devastating cattle viral diseases was officially declared globally eradicated in 2011 by FAO and OIE?
explanation: Rinderpest, also called cattle plague, was the first animal disease and only the second disease of any kind (after smallpox) to be globally eradicated. The Food and Agriculture Organization and the World Organisation for Animal Health jointly declared its eradication in June 2011, following decades of vaccination campaigns including India's Operation Rinderpest.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

options.text: Foot and Mouth Disease was declared globally eradicated
Bovine tuberculosis was declared globally eradicated
Brucellosis was declared globally eradicated
Rinderpest or cattle plague was declared globally eradicated

elimination_hints.hint: FMD is still endemic in many countries including India
Bovine TB persists worldwide and is not eradicated
Brucellosis remains a serious zoonosis worldwide

question_text: Brucellosis in cattle most characteristically causes which specific reproductive symptom in infected pregnant cows of the herd?
explanation: Brucellosis in cattle, caused by Brucella abortus, characteristically causes abortion in the last third of pregnancy along with retained placenta and reduced fertility. It is a major zoonosis transmitted to humans through unpasteurised milk and contact with infected birth products, causing undulant fever in humans.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

options.text: Permanent infertility right from the first heat cycle
Abortion in the last third of pregnancy with retained placenta
Production of triplet calves at every calving
Severe diarrhoea immediately after every calving

elimination_hints.hint: Brucellosis affects pregnancies, not first heat
Brucellosis causes loss, not multiple births
Diarrhoea is not the hallmark of brucellosis

question_text: Black Quarter or Black Leg disease of young cattle is caused by which specific anaerobic spore-forming bacterium?
explanation: Black Quarter (BQ), also called Black Leg, is caused by Clostridium chauvoei, an anaerobic, Gram-positive, spore-forming bacterium that lives in soil. Spores enter through wounds or are ingested and germinate in muscle tissue, producing toxins that cause hot, crepitating swellings of the heavy muscles of the hindquarters in young, well-nourished cattle.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

options.text: Mycobacterium bovis the cause of bovine tuberculosis
Brucella abortus the cause of contagious abortion
Clostridium chauvoei an anaerobic soil organism
Pasteurella multocida the cause of haemorrhagic septicaemia

elimination_hints.hint: Mycobacterium bovis causes TB, not BQ
Brucella causes abortion, not muscle swelling
Pasteurella causes HS with throat swelling, not muscle gangrene

question_text: Haemorrhagic Septicaemia (HS) in cattle and buffaloes typically becomes most prevalent during which specific climatic condition in India?
explanation: Haemorrhagic Septicaemia, caused by Pasteurella multocida serotype B:2, characteristically appears in epidemic form during the monsoon season in India. Stress from sudden temperature changes, heavy rains and waterlogging precipitate outbreaks, especially in buffaloes. Annual pre-monsoon vaccination with HS oil-adjuvant vaccine is the recommended preventive strategy.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

options.text: Peak summer with very low humidity in May
Monsoon season with high humidity and waterlogging
Severe winter with sub-zero temperatures only
Dry post-harvest season of late October

elimination_hints.hint: Hot dry weather is not the trigger for HS outbreaks
HS is rare in dry winter conditions
Post-harvest dryness is not the typical HS season

question_text: The California Mastitis Test (CMT) is a side-cow screening test that detects which specific component in the milk sample?
explanation: The California Mastitis Test detects somatic cells, mainly leukocytes, in milk by using an alkyl-aryl sulphonate reagent that disrupts cell membranes and gels with their DNA. The greater the gel formation, the higher the somatic cell count and hence the greater the udder inflammation. It is a quick cow-side indicator of subclinical mastitis.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

options.text: Total fat percentage of the milk sample tested
Somatic cells in milk indicating inflammation
Lactose content of the milk sample tested
Antibiotic residues remaining in the milk sample

elimination_hints.hint: Fat content is measured by Gerber method
Lactose is measured by chemical or instrumental methods
Antibiotic residues are detected by separate residue tests

question_text: Which of the following diseases of cattle is a serious zoonosis transmissible to humans through unpasteurised milk?
explanation: Brucellosis is a major zoonosis transmitted to humans most commonly by drinking unpasteurised milk from infected cows or buffaloes, and by direct contact with aborted material. In humans it causes undulant fever, joint pain and chronic ill-health. Pasteurisation of milk is therefore mandatory in dairy plants, and infected animals are tested and culled.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

options.text: Black Quarter disease primarily transmitted via soil only
Foot and Mouth Disease that spreads only between hooves
Brucellosis transmitted to humans via unpasteurised milk
Theileriosis spread only by ticks among cattle alone

elimination_hints.hint: BQ is not a milk-borne human disease
FMD rarely affects humans clinically
Theileriosis is not transmitted via milk

question_text: Why is it strictly recommended that carcasses of cattle dying suspected of anthrax should not be opened for post-mortem examination in the field?
explanation: Bacillus anthracis bacteria within an unopened carcass die quickly due to lack of oxygen. However, if the carcass is opened, the bacteria are exposed to air and form highly resistant spores that can contaminate the soil and persist for decades. The infected carcass should be buried deep with lime or burned, never opened in the field.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

options.text: Exposure to air leads to spore formation contaminating soil for decades
The carcass smell becomes unbearable for the field staff
Anthrax bacteria can fly out of the carcass quickly
Opening reduces the meat value of the carcass severely

elimination_hints.hint: Smell is not the public health concern
Bacteria do not fly; spores spread by contact
Anthrax carcass meat is unsafe for consumption anyway

question_text: Match the following important cattle diseases in Column A with their correct causal organisms listed in Column B.
explanation: Foot and Mouth Disease is caused by an Aphthovirus; Anthrax by Bacillus anthracis; Black Quarter by Clostridium chauvoei; and Haemorrhagic Septicaemia by Pasteurella multocida. Knowing the exact causal organism is essential for diagnosis, vaccine choice and treatment.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

elimination_hints.hint: FMD is viral, not Pasteurella
Anthrax is not caused by Pasteurella
FMD is viral, not bacterial like Bacillus

question_text: Match the following livestock diseases in Column A with their characteristic clinical signs given in Column B.
explanation: FMD shows vesicles on tongue and feet with profuse salivation; mastitis shows udder swelling and abnormal milk; brucellosis shows abortion in late pregnancy; and HS shows high fever with throat swelling and dyspnoea. Recognising these signs guides field diagnosis before laboratory confirmation.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

column_b.text: High fever with throat swelling and difficult breathing
Abortion in last trimester with retained placenta
Swollen udder with clots or blood in milk
Vesicles on tongue and feet with profuse salivation

elimination_hints.hint: FMD does not present with throat swelling primarily
FMD causes vesicles, not abortion
FMD vesicles are on tongue and feet, not udder

question_text: Match the cattle vaccines in Column A with the diseases they are used to prevent listed in Column B in India.
explanation: FMD vaccine prevents Foot and Mouth Disease; HS oil-adjuvant vaccine protects against Haemorrhagic Septicaemia; BQ vaccine prevents Black Quarter; and Anthrax spore vaccine prevents anthrax in endemic areas. These are the core vaccines under India's Livestock Health and Disease Control programme.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

elimination_hints.hint: FMD vaccine cannot prevent anthrax
FMD vaccine does not prevent BQ
Anthrax vaccine does not prevent HS

question_text: Read the assertion and reason about Foot and Mouth Disease control carefully and choose the most correct option below.
explanation: Both statements are true and the reason correctly explains the assertion. India has multiple co-circulating FMD serotypes O, A and Asia-1 with no cross-protection between them. Therefore the routine vaccine used under the National Animal Disease Control Programme is trivalent, covering all three locally important serotypes simultaneously to give broad herd immunity.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture
assertion: FMD vaccine used in India under the national programme is a trivalent inactivated vaccine.
reason: Three FMD serotypes O, A and Asia-1 circulate in India and there is no cross-protection between serotypes.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true

elimination_hints.hint: Lack of cross-protection between serotypes is the very reason for trivalent design
Three serotype circulation in India is well documented
Trivalent FMD vaccine in India is well established

question_text: Read the following assertion and reason about mastitis control on a dairy farm carefully and choose the option below.
explanation: Both statements are true and the reason correctly explains the assertion. Pre-milking teat dipping with disinfectant and post-milking teat dipping greatly reduce bacterial contamination of the teat canal during the time it remains open after milking. This breaks the cycle of bacterial entry into the udder, reducing the incidence of new mastitis infections in the herd.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture
assertion: Post-milking teat dipping in disinfectant significantly reduces the incidence of new mastitis infections.
reason: The teat canal remains open for some time after milking, and disinfection prevents bacterial entry.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true

elimination_hints.hint: Open teat canal is the very reason for post-milking dipping
The open-canal phenomenon is a well-known dairy fact
Reduction of mastitis by teat dipping is well documented

question_text: Read the assertion and reason about the bovine tuberculosis control programme and choose the most appropriate option.
explanation: The assertion is true but the reason is false. Bovine tuberculosis is controlled mainly by the test-and-cull strategy using tuberculin skin testing, because no fully effective vaccine is available for routine use in cattle and BCG interferes with the diagnostic skin test. Hence vaccination is not the global cornerstone of bovine TB control.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture
assertion: Bovine tuberculosis is controlled mainly by test-and-cull strategy using tuberculin skin testing.
reason: An effective vaccine for routine use in cattle is widely available and forms the cornerstone of TB control.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true

elimination_hints.hint: No effective routine TB vaccine exists in cattle
The reason itself is biologically false
Test-and-cull strategy is well established for bovine TB

question_text: Arrange the following steps in handling a suspected anthrax cattle death in correct chronological order in the field.
explanation: On finding a sudden cattle death with dark unclotted blood from natural orifices, the carcass must NOT be opened. A blood smear from the ear vein is taken for laboratory confirmation. The carcass is then disposed by deep burial with quick-lime or burnt. Finally, all in-contact animals are vaccinated and the area is cordoned off and disinfected.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

items.text: Vaccinate in-contact animals and disinfect the area
Suspect anthrax based on sudden death with unclotted blood
Take ear-vein blood smear for laboratory confirmation
Dispose of carcass by deep burial with lime or burning

options.text: Q → R → S → P
P → Q → R → S
S → P → Q → R
R → S → P → Q

elimination_hints.hint: Vaccination cannot precede recognition of disease
Disposal cannot precede suspicion and confirmation
Smear collection cannot precede the suspicion of anthrax

question_text: Arrange the following steps of the standard milking hygiene routine on a dairy farm in correct chronological order to prevent mastitis.
explanation: Good milking hygiene begins with washing and drying the udder with a clean cloth, followed by stripping the first few squirts of milk from each quarter to discard them and observe abnormalities. Pre-milking teat dipping with a disinfectant is then done. The cow is milked completely and finally each teat is dipped post-milking to seal it against bacterial entry.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

items.text: Wash and dry udder with clean cloth
Strip first few squirts of milk from each quarter

options.text: Q → R → S → P
P → S → R → Q
S → Q → P → R
R → P → Q → S

elimination_hints.hint: Post-milking dip cannot be the first step
Pre-milking dip cannot precede udder washing
Stripping cannot precede udder washing

question_text: State whether the following statement about rinderpest in India is TRUE or FALSE with justification.
explanation: The statement is TRUE. India officially declared itself free of rinderpest in 2006 under the OIE pathway, and the World Organisation for Animal Health and FAO declared global eradication in 2011. India's Operation Rinderpest used mass tissue culture rinderpest vaccine campaigns from the 1960s onwards to achieve this historic success.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture
statement: India officially declared itself free of rinderpest in 2006 under OIE pathway before global eradication in 2011.

options.text: TRUE — Operation Rinderpest with mass vaccination eliminated the disease from India
FALSE — rinderpest still occurs sporadically in remote Indian villages
FALSE — India never had rinderpest in its livestock at any point
FALSE — rinderpest was eradicated only in 2025 worldwide

elimination_hints.hint: Rinderpest no longer occurs anywhere globally
India had massive rinderpest outbreaks historically
Global eradication was declared in 2011, not 2025

question_text: Study the comparison table of major bacterial diseases of cattle in the diagram and answer the question that follows.
explanation: The diagram compares the causal organism, key clinical sign and routine vaccine for six major bacterial diseases of cattle. Black Quarter is caused by Clostridium chauvoei and presents as a hot crepitating swelling of the hindquarter, prevented by annual BQ vaccination. Other rows similarly distinguish anthrax, HS, brucellosis, TB and mastitis with their distinct organisms and signs.
correct_answer_text: Black Quarter is caused by Clostridium chauvoei with hot crepitating swelling of the hindquarter.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

options.text: Anthrax is caused by Clostridium chauvoei and prevented by BQ vaccine
Mastitis is routinely prevented by anthrax spore vaccine in dairy farms
Black Quarter is caused by Clostridium chauvoei and prevented by annual BQ vaccine
Bovine TB is routinely prevented by an effective live vaccine

elimination_hints.hint: Anthrax is caused by Bacillus anthracis, not Clostridium
Mastitis prevention is mainly by hygiene, not anthrax vaccine
No routine effective vaccine exists for bovine TB
